title: helleborus × lucidus
intro: Helleborus × lucidus, also known as Christmas rose or black hellebore, is a hybrid plant species within the Helleborus genus, characterized by its glossy, evergreen leaves and nodding, bell-shaped flowers that bloom in shades of white, pink, and purple. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of glycosides, alkaloids, and other bioactive compounds. Consuming hellebore extracts may offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving immune function, although its effects on the body can be toxic in large doses, making it essential to use it in moderation and under medical supervision. In herbal preparations, hellebore is often used in teas, tinctures, and ointments to treat various conditions, including colds, flu, and skin infections, although its use should be avoided during pregnancy and breastfeeding due to potential toxicity.
